caption:
The Harsh Reality of a Broken UK

The UK is now officially the second most depressing country to live in.

Taxes are at a post-war record high:

	•	Up to 45% income tax
	•	20% VAT
	•	25% corporation tax
	•	15% national insurance
	•	39 different taxes in total

Town centres are in ruin, high streets are empty, homelessness is rising, and crime is spiralling out of control.

The cost of living keeps soaring. Inflation has already wiped out 35% of your money in just five years.

You work 3 days out of 5 for the government, paying thousands in debt interest. When you finally retire, you face a 40% inheritance tax.

And it’s only getting worse…

	•	Digital ID is being pushed through
	•	Cash will soon disappear
	•	More taxes, more control, less freedom

People are leaving the UK or taking to the streets.

So why are you still here?
.
.
#uk #fyp #explore #london #britain #brokenbritain #england #dubai #didyouknow #foryou

I’m 36 years old and I’ve never been so stressed financially. I can’t sell my flat in London even though I don’t work or live there any more, I’m taxed more than I make from the rent, which is virtually nothing after mortgage, service charge and ground rent etc. then I had two jobs last year - worked full time PAYE and freelanced and the amount I was taxed on the freelancing made it almost pointless. I also had to pay 18 months at once as they take the full year + 6 months of the following year, based on the same income, even though my freelancing income halved so I had to pay more than I’d been able to save because it wasn’t based on what I’d actually earned…. Honestly, it’s all mad. Even people doing okay and working really hard can’t keep up.
